title: How The Liberty Global (LBTYA) Investment Story Is Shifting With Fresh Analyst Targets
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

The latest research refresh on Liberty Global nudges fair value estimates slightly higher, with one model moving from US$15.84 to US$15.89 per share and other commentary pointing to targets around US$14. These fine tuning moves are being framed by analysts as adjustments to existing views rather than a reset of the story, with modest changes in targets tied to refreshed assumptions after recent results. As you read on, you will see how these numbers feed into the evolving analyst narrative and what to watch next.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Liberty Global.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Morgan Stanley lifted its price target on Liberty Global to US$14 from US$13, which signals that its updated forecasts and valuation work support some upside versus earlier assumptions. Analysts highlighting targets in the mid teens, including the recent move to around US$14, point to a view that the current share price already embeds a fair amount of caution, leaving room if execution lines up with those refreshed models.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Morgan Stanley kept an Equal Weight rating, which indicates the firm does not see Liberty Global as clearly mispriced and instead views the risk and reward as fairly balanced at current levels. The modest 60 cent and US$1 adjustments in recent targets suggest that, while analysts are refining inputs after earnings, they are not treating the latest results as a clear thesis changer on growth prospects or valuation.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NasdaqGS:LBTY.A 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 2 risks for Liberty Global. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Liberty Global has tabled a bid for a European basketball team, highlighting interest in expanding around sports related assets alongside existing media exposure. Goldman Sachs is reported to be working with Liberty Global on a potential sale of its stake in Wyre, indicating ongoing portfolio reshaping efforts. The joint owners of Virgin Media O2, including Liberty Global, plan to acquire UK fibre operator Netomnia, which would add another broadband asset to that partnership. CK Hutchison Holdings is in advanced talks to sell Three Ireland to Liberty Global. If a deal is reached, this would give Liberty Global a larger telecom footprint in Ireland.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Liberty Global

Fair value per share updated from US$15.84 to US$15.89. Revenue growth assumption revised from 1.48% to 1.10%. Net profit margin adjusted from 12.84% to 12.89%. Future P/E multiple moved from 9.81x to 9.95x. Discount rate updated from 9.92% to 10.10%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives connect Liberty Global's business story to analysts' forecasts and fair value estimates, so you can see how the numbers line up with what the company is doing. They refresh as new data, deals, and risks are added.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on Liberty Global to stay up to date on:

How fiber and DOCSIS 4.0 upgrades, along with network sharing deals in markets like Belgium and the UK, are aimed at supporting high speed broadband and margins. Plans for corporate simplification, digital transformation, and potential asset spin offs that are intended to reduce costs and address any conglomerate discount. Key risks such as intense telecom competition, regulatory scrutiny on pricing and network sharing, and higher leverage at some operating companies.
